vulnerability.reason: Both describe public inability to discern AI-generated videos due to lack of forensic literacy and over-reliance on visual realism.
vulnerability.reference: Low public literacy in AI-video forensics enables reliance on superficial realism cues for credibility assessment.
vulnerability.generated: Public media illiteracy and cognitive trust in visual evidence as inherently truthful prevent discernment of AI-generated fabrications.

impact.reason: Generated includes erosion of shared factual reality but omits degradation of public trust in all video media, replacing it with manipulation of public beliefs.
impact.reference: Erosion of shared factual reality, degrading public trust in all video media.
impact.generated: Erosion of shared factual reality and manipulation of public beliefs through viral misinformation.

dose_response.reason: Both arcs describe physically implausible video from a decoder-only transformer, public consumption on social media, exploitation of low media literacy/trust, leading to false beliefs and erosion of reality.
dose_response.reference: Decoder-only transformers generate physically implausible videos, which, when consumed by the public on social media, bypass heuristic credibility checks, leading to false belief formation.
dose_response.generated: Decoder-only transformer generating coherent but physically implausible video, through public consumption on social media, triggers media illiteracy and trust in visual truth, leading to erosion of shared factual reality and manipulation of public beliefs via viral misinformation.
